title: Containerization: Dockerfiles for Discovery and Sentinel, Minimal Images, SBOM Generation, Image Signing
description: Create production-grade Dockerfiles for the Discovery Service, Sentinel, and Discovery UI using multi-stage builds to minimize image size. Images must run as non-root, include healthchecks, and be built reproducibly. Integrate supply-chain security controls: generate SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) in CycloneDX format during CI builds, and sign images with cosign. Provide a docker-compose override for the full local development stack.
objectives: Multi-stage Dockerfile for Discovery Service (Python): build wheels stage + slim runtime stage
Multi-stage Dockerfile for Sentinel (Python): same pattern, include sentinelctl CLI
Multi-stage Dockerfile for Discovery UI (React/Vite): Node build stage + nginx serve stage
All images run as non-root (UID 1001), expose ports > 1024, include HEALTHCHECK
Generate CycloneDX SBOM via syft during CI and attach to release artifacts
Sign images with cosign (keyless in CI via OIDC) and verify in deploy pipeline

steps.title: Discovery Service Dockerfile (multi-stage Python)
steps.detail: File: `infra/docker/discovery/Dockerfile`. Stage 1 (builder): `FROM python:3.12-slim AS builder`. `WORKDIR /build`. Copy `pyproject.toml`, `requirements.lock`. Run `pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock --no-cache-dir --target /build/packages`. Stage 2 (runtime): `FROM python:3.12-slim AS runtime`. `RUN adduser -u 1001 -D -s /sbin/nologin appuser`. `WORKDIR /app`. Copy `/build/packages` to `/app/packages`. Set `ENV PYTHONPATH=/app/packages`. Copy application source. `RUN chown -R appuser:appuser /app`. `USER appuser`. Expose port 8000. `HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=20s --retries=3 CMD python -c "import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:8000/health/live')" || exit 1`. `CMD ["uvicorn", "discovery.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000", "--workers", "1"]`.

steps.title: Sentinel Dockerfile (multi-stage Python with CLI)
steps.detail: File: `infra/docker/sentinel/Dockerfile`. Same multi-stage pattern as Discovery. Additional: copy `$SENTINEL_HOME` as a volume mount point (create empty dir). Include `sentinelctl` CLI by copying `sentinel/cli/sentinelctl.py` and its entrypoint. In stage 2: copy the wheels for sentinelctl dependencies. Expose port 8080. In sentinel container, the `$SENTINEL_HOME` directory must be a named volume (not baked into image) to persist keys across restarts. `ENV SENTINEL_HOME=/sentinel-home`. Create `/sentinel-home` directory with `RUN mkdir -p /sentinel-home && chown appuser:appuser /sentinel-home`. VOLUME /sentinel-home.

steps.title: Discovery UI Dockerfile (Node build + nginx serve)
steps.detail: File: `infra/docker/discovery-ui/Dockerfile`. Stage 1 (builder): `FROM node:20-alpine AS builder`. `WORKDIR /build`. Copy `package.json`, `package-lock.json`. Run `npm ci --omit=dev`. Copy source. Run `npm run build` (outputs to `dist/`). Stage 2 (runtime): `FROM nginx:1.25-alpine AS runtime`. Copy `infra/docker/discovery-ui/nginx.conf` to `/etc/nginx/nginx.conf`. Copy `/build/dist` to `/usr/share/nginx/html`. `RUN adduser -u 1001 -D nginx-user && chown -R nginx-user:nginx-user /usr/share/nginx/html /var/cache/nginx /var/log/nginx`. `USER nginx-user`. Port 8080 (nginx listens on 8080 as non-root — default 80 requires root). `HEALTHCHECK CMD wget -qO- http://localhost:8080/ || exit 1`.

steps.title: nginx.conf for Discovery UI
steps.detail: File: `infra/docker/discovery-ui/nginx.conf`. Key settings: listen 8080; root /usr/share/nginx/html; server_name _. For SPA routing: `try_files $uri /index.html;` (handles React Router paths). Security headers: `add_header X-Frame-Options DENY; add_header X-Content-Type-Options nosniff; add_header Referrer-Policy no-referrer; add_header Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; connect-src 'self' {API_BASE};"`. Gzip: `gzip on; gzip_types text/css application/javascript application/json`. Cache static assets: `location ~* \.(js|css|png|ico|woff2)$ { expires 1y; add_header Cache-Control 'public, immutable'; }`. No server-side file listing.

steps.title: SBOM generation with syft
steps.detail: In CI pipeline (TASK-064), after each Docker build: run `syft <image_name> -o cyclonedx-json > sbom-<component>.json`. Attach SBOM as CI artifact and associate with GitHub Release. For Docker Hub or ECR: attach SBOM using `cosign attach sbom --sbom sbom-<component>.json <image_ref>`. Install syft: `curl -sSfL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anchore/syft/main/install.sh | sh -s -- -b /usr/local/bin`. Run before signing. SBOM captures: all Python packages in image, system packages from apt/apk, and metadata (image digest, build time).

steps.title: Image signing with cosign
steps.detail: Use keyless signing in CI via Sigstore OIDC (GitHub Actions OIDC token). In CI: `cosign sign --yes <image_digest>` after push. This signs with a certificate issued by Fulcio (Sigstore's CA) and logs to Rekor transparency log. In deploy pipeline: verify before pulling: `cosign verify --certificate-identity-regexp=<github_actions_URL> --certificate-oidc-issuer=https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com <image_digest>`. Install cosign: `go install github.com/sigstore/cosign/v2/cmd/cosign@latest` (or use pre-built binary). For air-gapped environments where Sigstore is unavailable: use key-based signing with `cosign sign --key cosign.key` (key stored in CI secrets).

steps.title: Local development docker-compose
steps.detail: File: `infra/docker/docker-compose.dev.yml` extending base `docker-compose.yml` (TASK-010). Add `sentinel-producer` and `sentinel-consumer` services: use built images or `build: context: .` for local build. Add volumes for `sentinel-home-producer` and `sentinel-home-consumer` (named volumes). Add `discovery-ui` service using the UI Dockerfile. Override env vars for dev: `SENTINEL_UI_ENABLED=true`, `SENTINEL_UI_AUTH=none`, `DISCOVERY_UI_OIDC_AUTHORITY=http://keycloak:8080/realms/discovery` (keycloak container for local OIDC). Network: all services on `sentinel_net` internal network.

steps.title: Image size and security validation
steps.detail: After build: run `docker image inspect --format '{{.Size}}' <image>` and fail CI if Discovery > 300MB or Sentinel > 300MB or UI > 50MB. Run `trivy image --exit-code 1 --severity HIGH,CRITICAL <image>` to scan for vulnerabilities in base image and dependencies. Trivy should be run AFTER pip install --require-hashes to ensure scanned packages match what's installed. Fail CI on any CRITICAL or HIGH CVE in final image. Document: which base image version is used, schedule for base image update (monthly or on CVE disclosure).

security_constraints: All images run as UID 1001 — confirmed via `docker run --user check` in CI validation
Base images pinned to digest in production Dockerfiles: `FROM python:3.12-slim@sha256:...` — prevents supply-chain attack via floating tag mutation
No SSH keys, API tokens, or .env files copied into image — enforced by .dockerignore
HEALTHCHECK must exercise the actual health endpoint (not just `echo 'alive'`)
Image must not contain pip, setuptools residue from build stage in runtime stage — multi-stage ensures separation
acceptance_criteria: All images run as UID 1001 (confirmed with docker inspect)
SBOM generated and attached to CI artifacts for each build
cosign verify succeeds on signed images in deploy pipeline
trivy scan finds no CRITICAL CVEs in final images
